questions.question: The diagram shows different types of epithelial tissues. Identify the pseudostratified ciliated columnar epithelium.
questions.diagram_description: Four diagrams of epithelial tissue cross-sections: (A) single layer of flat cells; (B) single layer of tall column-shaped cells with cilia at apical surface, nuclei at different heights giving false appearance of stratification; (C) multiple distinct layers of cells; (D) single layer of cube-shaped cells

questions.question: In cockroach, the malpighian tubules open into:
Which of the following correctly describes the nervous tissue?
Study the diagram of a sarcomere showing different bands. What is the region between two Z-lines called?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of sarcomere showing: Z-line, I-band (actin only), A-band (actin+myosin overlap), H-zone (myosin only), M-line in center, with labels indicating thin (actin) and thick (myosin) filaments

questions.question: Which type of epithelium is found in the urinary bladder?
Cockroach belongs to order:
The compound eyes of cockroach provide:
The diagram shows the internal anatomy of a frog. Identify the organ that serves BOTH digestive and excretory functions.
questions.diagram_description: Ventral dissection diagram of frog showing: oral cavity, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine, liver (large three-lobed), gall bladder, pancreas, kidneys (dorsal), fat bodies, gonads, urinary bladder, cloaca

questions.question: In frog, the skin acts as a respiratory organ. This type of respiration is called:
Neuroglia cells are found in:
Which tissue is characterized by intercalated discs?
The diagram shows the cross-section of bone tissue. Identify the structure that connects adjacent Haversian systems.
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of compact bone showing: Haversian canal at center, concentric lamellae around it, osteocytes in lacunae, canaliculi connecting lacunae, Volkmann's canals running perpendicular to Haversian canals, periosteum on outside

questions.question: Stratified squamous epithelium is found in:
The ground substance of connective tissue is secreted by:
In cockroach, how many spiracles are present on each side?
The diagram shows types of cartilage. In which location is fibrocartilage found?
questions.diagram_description: Three diagrams of cartilage types showing matrix composition: (A) Hyaline – clear glassy matrix with scattered chondrocytes; (B) Elastic – matrix with elastic fibres; (C) Fibrocartilage – matrix with dense collagen fibres, chondrocytes in rows between fibre bundles. Locations for each type indicated

questions.question: Which type of muscle contraction does NOT require calcium ions?
The reproductive organs of male cockroach are located in which segments?
Proleg is found in which of the following insects?
Which of the following is NOT a component of areolar connective tissue?
The diagram of cockroach's nervous system shows nerve ganglia. How many ganglia are present in the abdominal region?
questions.diagram_description: Ventral nervous system of cockroach showing: supra-oesophageal ganglion (brain) in head, sub-oesophageal ganglion, 3 thoracic ganglia in thoracic segments, 6 abdominal ganglia in abdomen, all connected by double ventral nerve cord

questions.question: Microvilli are found on the apical surface of:
Which cells are found in blood that are nucleated in frog but enucleated in humans?
Dense regular connective tissue is found in:
The anal cerci in cockroach help in:
Which of the following is a multicellular exocrine gland that opens into a duct?
In frog, the renal portal system carries blood from:
Which type of epithelium forms the thyroid follicles?
The diagram shows a neuron. Which part is responsible for receiving signals from other neurons?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of multipolar neuron showing: cell body (soma) with nucleus, multiple branching dendrites extending from soma, single long axon with myelin sheath, nodes of Ranvier, axon terminals (synaptic knobs), arrows indicating direction of impulse

questions.question: The crop of cockroach functions as:
Which connective tissue has maximum tensile strength?
Meissner's corpuscles are:
The hepatic caecae in cockroach are part of:
Which of the following cells releases histamine during allergic reactions?
The diagram shows the cross-section of skeletal muscle fibre. What is the functional unit of contraction?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section and longitudinal section of skeletal muscle showing: muscle fibre with multiple peripheral nuclei, myofibrils running longitudinally, sarcomere between Z-lines, actin (thin filaments) and myosin (thick filaments), sarcoplasmic reticulum surrounding myofibrils, T-tubules at A-I junction

questions.question: Which type of connective tissue forms the stroma of lymph nodes?
Mesothorax of cockroach bears:

options.A: First pair of legs and tegmina
options.B: Second pair of legs and tegmina
options.C: Third pair of legs and hindwings
options.D: Second pair of legs and hindwings

questions.question: Fat body, nephrocytes and urecose glands also help in excretion in cockroach.
Blood (haemolymph) of cockroach is colorless because it lacks ______.
Nervous system of cockroach consists of series of fused, segmentally arranged ______.
Mushroom gland is present in the ______ reproductive system of cockroach.
Ootheca of cockroach is a capsule containing ______ eggs.
Development of cockroach is paurometabolous (gradual development through nymphs).
Compound eyes of cockroach consist of about 2000 hexagonal ______.
Frogs have ______ chambered heart.
Excretory product of frog is ______.
Mesovarium is a fold of peritoneum that suspends ______ in frogs.
Vasa efferentia in male frog enter the kidneys through ______ canal.
Sensory papillae on frog skin help in touch.
Frog's tongue is bilobed.
Ear in cockroach? no. they have sound receptors on legs and cerci.
Anal cerci are present in both male and female cockroaches.
Anal styles are present only in ______ cockroaches.
Forewings of cockroach are called ______.
Hindwings of cockroach are used in flight.
The abdomen of both males and females cockroach consists of ______ segments.
In female cockroach, 7th sternum is boat-shaped.
Circulatory system? no. earthworm has closed circulatory system.
Frogs use ______ for respiration on land.
Mosaic vision in cockroach? yes. it has more sensitivity but less resolution.
Titillator is part of male reproductive system in cockroach.
Chilling? no. replaced: Spermatozoa are stored in spermatheca of female cockroach after mating.
Tracheal tubes in cockroach are kept open by chitinous rings.
Sclerites? yes. they are the plates of exoskeleton.
Tergites? yes. dorsal sclerites.
Sternites? yes. ventral sclerites.
Arthrodial membrane? yes. thin flexible membrane joining sclerites.
Macrophage? yes. phagocytic cell of connective tissue.
Fibroblast? yes. cell that produces fibers.
Heart of frog lacks p-valves? no. it has valves to prevent backflow.
Cloaca in frogs is common chamber for feces, urine and gametes.
The 'Tight Junctions' in epithelial tissues serve what primary function?

options.A: Stop substances from leaking across a tissue

questions.question: Which of the following describes the 'Compound Epithelium'?

options.A: Multi-layered with limited role in secretion and absorption

The visual units of the compound eyes in cockroaches are known as:
